common_collocations: onze ans, onze heures, onze personnes
image_context: The number 11 displayed clearly on a white background
example_2: Le magasin ouvre à onze heures.
example_2_translation: The store opens at eleven o'clock.
memory_hook: Onze = one-z, eleven has 11 letters sound
related_words: dix, douze, treize, le nombre
common_mistakes: Onze doesn't change form for gender or number

common_collocations: douze mois, douze heures, douze jours
image_context: The number 12 displayed clearly on a white background
example_1: Il y a douze mois dans une année.
example_1_translation: There are twelve months in a year.
example_2: Nous sommes douze dans ma classe.
example_2_translation: We are twelve in my class.
memory_hook: Douze = dozen, twelve things in a dozen
related_words: onze, treize, dix, le nombre
common_mistakes: Douze doesn't change; same form always

common_collocations: treize ans, treize heures, treize personnes
image_context: The number 13 displayed clearly on a white background
example_1: Mon anniversaire est le treize mai.
example_1_translation: My birthday is the thirteenth of May.
example_2: Il a treize ans.
example_2_translation: He is thirteen years old.
memory_hook: Treize = tree-z, thirteen trees in a forest
related_words: douze, quatorze, le nombre, l'anniversaire
common_mistakes: Treize doesn't change; same form always

common_collocations: quatorze jours, quatorze ans, quatorze heures
image_context: The number 14 displayed clearly on a white background
example_2: Elle a quatorze ans maintenant.
example_2_translation: She is fourteen years old now.
memory_hook: Quatorze = fourteen, four + ten
related_words: treize, quinze, dix, le nombre
common_mistakes: Quatorze doesn't change; same form always

common_collocations: quinze minutes, quinze ans, quinze euros
image_context: The number 15 displayed clearly on a white background
example_2: J'ai quinze euros dans mon portefeuille.
example_2_translation: I have fifteen euros in my wallet.
memory_hook: Quinze = fifteen, five times three
related_words: quatorze, seize, dix, le nombre
common_mistakes: Quinze doesn't change; same form always

common_collocations: seize ans, seize heures, seize jours
image_context: The number 16 displayed clearly on a white background
example_1: Il a seize ans, il peut conduire.
example_1_translation: He is sixteen years old, he can drive.
example_2: La fête commence à seize heures.
example_2_translation: The party starts at four o'clock.
memory_hook: Seize = say-z, sixteen sayings
related_words: quinze, dix-sept, dix, le nombre
common_mistakes: Seize doesn't change; same form always

common_collocations: dix-sept ans, dix-sept heures, dix-sept personnes
image_context: The number 17 displayed clearly on a white background
example_1_translation: My sister is seventeen years old.
example_2: Le film commence à dix-sept heures.
example_2_translation: The film starts at five o'clock.
memory_hook: Dix-sept = dix (10) + sept (7) = 17
related_words: seize, dix-huit, dix, le nombre
common_mistakes: Use hyphen to connect the two parts

common_collocations: dix-huit ans, dix-huit heures, dix-huit jours
image_context: The number 18 displayed clearly on a white background
example_1_translation: I am eighteen years old now.
example_2: Dix-huit personnes sont venues.
example_2_translation: Eighteen people came.
example_3: Le bus arrive à dix-huit heures.
example_3_translation: The bus arrives at six o'clock.
memory_hook: Dix-huit = dix (10) + huit (8) = 18
related_words: dix-sept, dix-neuf, dix, le nombre
common_mistakes: Use hyphen between dix and huit

common_collocations: dix-neuf ans, dix-neuf heures, dix-neuf personnes
image_context: The number 19 displayed clearly on a white background
example_1: Le concert est à dix-neuf heures.
example_1_translation: The concert is at seven o'clock.
example_2: Il reste dix-neuf places.
example_2_translation: There are nineteen places left.
memory_hook: Dix-neuf = dix (10) + neuf (9) = 19
related_words: dix-huit, vingt, dix, le nombre
common_mistakes: Use hyphen between dix and neuf

common_collocations: vingt ans, vingt heures, vingt personnes
image_context: The number 20 displayed clearly on a white background
example_2: Vingt étudiants sont dans la classe.
example_2_translation: Twenty students are in the class.
example_3_translation: The bus will arrive in twenty minutes.
memory_hook: Vingt = van, twenty of something
related_words: dix-neuf, vingt-un, dix, le nombre
common_mistakes: Vingt doesn't change; same form always

usage_notes: Adjective; agrees with gender and number
common_collocations: une fleur violette, une robe violette, des fleurs violettes
image_context: A bunch of fresh purple lavender flowers
example_2: J'aime la couleur violette.
example_2_translation: I like the color purple.
pronunciation_tips: Sounds like 'vyo-lay'; the 't' is mostly silent
memory_hook: Violet = the color of violets, a purple flower
related_words: la couleur, la fleur, pourpre, bleu
common_mistakes: Must agree with gender: violet (masc), violette (fem)

usage_notes: Invariable color adjective (stays same for all genders)
common_collocations: une robe orange, une couleur orange, des oranges
image_context: A bright orange citrus fruit on a white background
example_2: Il porte une chemise orange.
example_2_translation: He is wearing an orange shirt.
pronunciation_tips: Sounds like 'or-onzh'; the final 'e' is silent
memory_hook: Orange = the color of the orange fruit
related_words: la couleur, le fruit, jaune, rouge
common_mistakes: Orange is invariable as color; doesn't change form

usage_notes: Invariable color adjective (stays same for all genders)
common_collocations: un sac marron, des yeux marrons, une robe marron
image_context: Rich brown wooden planks showing natural grain texture
example_2: J'ai un sac marron.
example_2_translation: I have a brown bag.
pronunciation_tips: Sounds like 'mar-on'; the final 'n' is nasal
memory_hook: Marron = brown, like the color of chestnuts (marrons)
related_words: la couleur, le bois, brun, le marron
common_mistakes: Marron is invariable as color; doesn't change form

usage_notes: Invariable adjective; stays same for all genders
common_collocations: un ciel gris, des yeux gris, une robe grise
image_context: A gray overcast sky with clouds covering the sun
example_2: J'ai des cheveux gris.
example_2_translation: I have gray hair.
memory_hook: Gris = gray, the color of storm clouds
related_words: la couleur, le ciel, blanc, noir
common_mistakes: Gris is invariable as color; same form for all

usage_notes: Masculine noun; pet or wild animal
common_collocations: un lapin blanc, le lapin mange, mon lapin
image_context: A cute white rabbit with long ears sitting in grass
example_2: J'ai un lapin comme animal de compagnie.
example_2_translation: I have a rabbit as a pet.
example_3_translation: The rabbit jumps in the grass.
pronunciation_tips: Sounds like 'la-pan'; the final 'n' is nasal
memory_hook: Lapin = lap-in, rabbits lap up carrots
related_words: l'animal, la carotte, le lièvre, le rongeur
common_mistakes: Lapin is masculine (le lapin), not feminine

common_collocations: monter à cheval, un cheval noir, les chevaux
image_context: A beautiful brown horse standing in a green meadow
example_1: Le cheval court dans le pré.
example_1_translation: The horse runs in the field.
example_2: Elle monte à cheval le week-end.
example_2_translation: She rides horses on the weekend.
memory_hook: Cheval = horse, a majestic animal
related_words: l'animal, la selle, l'équitation, monter
common_mistakes: Cheval is masculine; plural is chevaux (not chevales)

usage_notes: Feminine noun; farm animal that gives milk
common_collocations: une vache blanche, la vache donne du lait
image_context: A black and white cow standing in a green pasture
example_2: Nous voyons des vaches dans la ferme.
example_2_translation: We see cows on the farm.
memory_hook: Vache = cow, gives milk for dairy
related_words: l'animal, la ferme, le lait, le troupeau
common_mistakes: Vache is feminine (la vache), not masculine

common_collocations: un cochon rose, le cochon aime la boue
image_context: A pink pig rolling in the mud in a farmyard
example_2: Nous avons un cochon à la ferme.
example_2_translation: We have a pig on the farm.
memory_hook: Cochon = pig, sounds like the oinking sound
related_words: l'animal, la ferme, le porc, la porcherie
common_mistakes: Cochon is masculine (le cochon), not feminine

usage_notes: Masculine noun; star at center of solar system
common_collocations: au soleil, le soleil brille, sous le soleil
image_context: A bright sun shining in a clear blue sky
example_2: J'aime me réchauffer au soleil.
example_2_translation: I like to warm myself in the sun.
example_3: Le soleil se couche le soir.
example_3_translation: The sun sets in the evening.
pronunciation_tips: Sounds like 'so-lay'; the final 'l' is pronounced
memory_hook: Soleil = sun, sole star in the sky
related_words: la lumière, la chaleur, l'été, la journée
common_mistakes: Soleil is masculine (le soleil), not feminine

usage_notes: Feminine noun; uncountable; water falling from clouds
common_collocations: sous la pluie, dans la pluie, la pluie tombe
image_context: Rain falling from dark clouds onto a wet street
example_2: J'ai besoin d'un parapluie sous la pluie.
example_2_translation: I need an umbrella in the rain.
memory_hook: Pluie = rain, sounds like water falling
related_words: le parapluie, la goutte, l'averse, l'eau
common_mistakes: Pluie is feminine (la pluie), not masculine

common_collocations: dans la neige, la neige blanche, la neige tombe
image_context: Fresh white snow covering trees and ground in a winter forest
example_1: La neige couvre le sol en hiver.
example_1_translation: The snow covers the ground in winter.
example_2: Les enfants jouent dans la neige.
example_2_translation: The children play in the snow.
example_3: La neige est froide et blanche.
example_3_translation: The snow is cold and white.
memory_hook: Neige = snow, covers the earth in white
related_words: l'hiver, le froid, le flocon, le glaçon
common_mistakes: Neige is feminine (la neige), not masculine

common_collocations: le vent souffle, un vent froid, dans le vent
image_context: Strong wind blowing through trees with leaves flying
example_1_translation: The wind is blowing hard today.
example_2: J'aime le vent frais du matin.
example_2_translation: I like the fresh morning wind.
example_3_translation: The wind blows away my hat.
pronunciation_tips: Sounds like 'von'; the final 't' is silent
memory_hook: Vent = wind, sounds like the air moving
related_words: la brise, l'air, la rafale, l'ouragan
common_mistakes: Vent is masculine (le vent), not feminine

usage_notes: Masculine noun; mass of water vapor in sky
common_collocations: un nuage blanc, le ciel, les nuages
image_context: Fluffy white clouds floating in a bright blue sky
example_1: Il y a beaucoup de nuages dans le ciel.
example_1_translation: There are many clouds in the sky.
example_2: Le nuage est blanc et léger.
example_2_translation: The cloud is white and light.
pronunciation_tips: Sounds like 'noo-azh'; the 'ua' is pronounced as one sound
memory_hook: Nuage = cloud, floats in the sky
related_words: le ciel, le temps, la pluie, le brouillard
common_mistakes: Nuage is masculine (le nuage), not feminine
